articles.title: Heatwave intensifies as temperatures near 46°C - The Hans India
articles.search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Vijayawada: With temperatures soaring across the state, Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority managing director Prakhar Jain has urged citizens to remain cautious as heatwave conditions intensify day by day.
According to officials, a maximum temperature of 45.7°C was recorded on Monday at Kambham in Markapuram district, one of the highest in the state. Out of 28 districts, as many as 227 mandals across 20 districts recorded temperatures exceeding 41°C, indicating widespread heatwave conditions.
The authority has warned that on Tuesday severe heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in 19 mandals, while heatwave conditions may affect another 21 mandals. Districts expected to witness severe heatwave impact include parts of Vizianagaram district and Parvathipuram Manyam district.
Meanwhile, due to a trough extending from Rayalaseema to Kanyakumari in Tamil Nadu, light rains accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning are likely in isolated places across districts such as districts of Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Prakasam, Nellore Kurnool and Nandyal.
Officials cautioned farmers and cattle herders to avoid taking shelter under trees during lightning and thunderstorm activity, as it can be extremely dangerous.
Several regions reported extreme temperatures on Monday, including 45.7°C at Kambham, 45.1°C at Kommipadu in SPSR Nellore district, 44.8°C in Prakasam district, and 44.2°C in Amaravati and parts of Tirupati and YSR Kadapa districts. Temperatures above 42°C were also recorded in multiple districts including Kurnool, NTR, Kakinada, Sri Sathya Sai, and Anantapur.
Prakhar Jain advised people to avoid venturing outdoors during peak afternoon hours unless absolutely necessary. He especially cautioned individuals with heart conditions, diabetes, and blood pressure issues to remain indoors during extreme heat.
He also stressed the importance of staying hydrated by drinking sufficient water regularly, even if not thirsty. People stepping out have been advised to wear protective gear such as sunglasses and take precautions to shield themselves from hot winds.
Authorities reiterated that heightened awareness and preventive measures are essential to minimise the risks associated with extreme heatwave conditions currently prevailing across the state.
